title: [Bug]: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over btrfs
body:
### Issue Description

Trying to run any podman command results in
```
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over btrfs at "/home/typecasto/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run any podman command.

### Describe the results you received

```
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over btrfs at "/home/typecasto/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman command works as intended

### podman info output

```shell
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over btrfs at "/home/typecasto/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

- OS is Arch Linux on x86-64
- Filesystem is BTRFS
- Podman is version 4.3.1-2

### Additional information

Happens as both root and normal user. Sorry I can't give much more info than what I've provided here, I usually try to be pretty thorough but I don't have much to go off of besides an error message.

comments.body: Rebooting fixed the issue. Sorry for submitting without testing that, I had already typed the issue and didn't want to have to retype it.
